{"step_by_step_thinking": "The query is about a MO cavity prepared for inlay and the mechanical advantage to prevent proximal displacement of the restoration. Option A, adequate pulpal depth, is not directly related to the mechanical advantage in the context of a restoration. Option B, parallel walls, could help distribute forces evenly but does not directly prevent proximal displacement. Option D, acute lingual inclination in axiogingival area, is not typically associated with preventing proximal displacement. Option C, occlusal dovetail, is a feature that helps lock the restoration in place and prevents it from dislodging, thus providing the mechanical advantage needed.", "answer_choice": "C"}
